rule0_reads.git_hash: n/a (research/Stage-1 hypothesis-generation code; no equity/DD/risk-control comparisons; per §0 conditional, null finding recorded)
n/a (no equity/DD/risk-control comparisons; categorization/reporting only; dd_wrapper is NotImplementedError stub)

adr_precision_rule_quote: Validator at 2 dp is correct for money math: floor, target, equity all live at the cent scale, and round(x, 2) matches the natural quantum of the variable. dd_from_peak is a ratio, not money. It has no cent quantum. The natural fineness is the smallest drawdown distinction the trader cares about — empirically, no DD policy resolves below 1 bp (10^-4). Rounding finer than 1 bp is sufficient. 6 dp = ~10^-6 = micro-percent precision. Eight orders of magnitude above float64 ULP at 0.015. Two orders of magnitude finer than any DD policy. Sufficient to collapse ULP noise without affecting any decision the trader could meaningfully care about.

findings.notes: Canonical fix site from PR #53; ALREADY ROUNDED per ADR 2026-05-10. Included as reference baseline for the pattern. LHS dd_from_peak is computed via (peak - equity) / peak (line 89), subtraction at near-equal magnitudes — exactly the ULP-vulnerable arithmetic the ADR addresses.

dd_from_start computed at line 181 as (STARTING_EQUITY - equity) / STARTING_EQUITY — same subtraction-at-near-equal-magnitudes pattern as dd_from_peak. RHS is a literal ratio. Comparison gates a DD warning printout ("HALT ALL TRADING" advisory at >=4%). Risk-control category per §4 ("drawdown check"); display surface but operationally advisory. Not yet rounded.

Second branch of the same dd_from_start ladder. Same arithmetic provenance and ULP-class as line 202. Advisory "REVIEW BEFORE TRADING" printout. Per §0.5 (4), counted as a distinct site (one operator instance per row).

findings.rhs: state["peak_equity"] (float from JSON state; previously computed/persisted)
findings.notes: Peak update path. LHS is raw user input (parsed float); RHS is a stored float (previously stored via JSON round-trip — itself a precision-mutating step). No arithmetic at the comparison frame, but JSON serialization can introduce ULP-class noise. Result feeds line 92 indirectly (peak governs dd_from_peak). Borderline: §4 says "pure variable references...excluded UNLESS one was previously computed" — state value qualifies as previously computed. RHS is not a constant threshold, so site partially fails the §4 schema. Classified `unclear` per §5 (4) discipline.

dd_remaining_pct is the property at line 28-32: round(self.dd_limit_pct - used_pct, 2) where used_pct = (lost / initial_balance) * 100. LHS is already rounded to 2 dp inside the property. Compared to integer 0 (RHS). Flags "ACCOUNT FAILED". Account-state-transition risk-control. Already-rounded; pattern-conformant but at 2dp not 6dp. Var-type ambiguous: dd_remaining_pct is a percentage-point value (0..5 scale, e.g. 5.0 = 5%), so it's a percentage-as-float (ratio) by §4 classification; but the in-file precision treatment is 2dp (the cent-quantum that matches the dd_limit_pct constants like 5.0). Not the canonical 6dp recommended for ratio per ADR — flagging as a precision-rule mismatch for parent review.
Same property as line 43. Flags "DD WARNING". Risk-control advisory. Same 2dp vs 6dp precision-rule mismatch flag — see line 43 notes.

target_remaining is property at line 35-38: round(target_balance - self.balance, 2). LHS already rounded to 2 dp (money/cents quantum, matches ADR money rule). Composite expression per §0.5 (4) — this row is the first operator only; second operator (self.profit_target_pct > 0) excluded as a sentinel-zero check on a non-arithmetic LHS. Flags "TARGET HIT". Account-state-positive, but counted per §4 ("account-state transition").

Auto-fail account-state transition path in update_balance(). Same property as lines 43/45. Composite per §0.5 (4); second operator (`a.phase != "failed"`) is `!=` and excluded from the in-scope operator set. Concrete account-state transition: phase->"failed".

findings.lhs_var: abs_losses (pandas Series of |loss| dollars; element-wise compare)
findings.notes: Vectorized element-wise comparison selecting 1%+ losses as full-stop cohort. Drives implied_1r calibration which underlies the locked MC anchor (98.09/0.36/4.73 at C2). Per §4 — gates a risk-control-adjacent decision (1R calibration; n<5 fallback case is named in user memory portfolio_mc_1r_fallback_trap.md as a ~10pp swing in MC results). LHS elements are pandas-loaded floats from CSV, not arithmetic-derived in this frame; ULP-vulnerability low for this specific compare but the boundary case (loss exactly equal to 2000.0) is plausible enough to flag `unclear`. Money-typed (LHS is dollars); ADR 2dp rule applies if treated.

MC simulation mirror of dd_protection.py:92 trigger compare. ALREADY ROUNDED per Q-MCFP-1 (commit 54d2285 in this same panel push) — included as a reference instance of the pattern. dd_from_peak here computed at line 196 as (eq - peak) / peak (note sign convention: this is negative, hence compare to -dd_trigger). Risk-control: gates DD-protection scaler inside the MC.
Daily-loss bust gate inside MC simulation. ALREADY ROUNDED per Q-MCFP-1. Reference instance. Risk-control: terminates a sim with bust_daily outcome.
Static-DD bust gate inside MC simulation. ALREADY ROUNDED per Q-MCFP-1. Reference instance. Risk-control: terminates a sim with bust_static outcome.

findings.expression: if round(eq, 2) >= PROFIT_TARGET and trade_days >= MIN_TRADING_DAYS:
findings.notes: Profit-target pass gate inside MC simulation. ALREADY ROUNDED at 2 dp (money/cents). Composite per §0.5 (4); second operator (`trade_days >= MIN_TRADING_DAYS`) excluded — trade_days is int. Reference instance: matches ADR money-math 2dp rule.
Mirror of line 198 inside mode_historical (deterministic walk). ALREADY ROUNDED per Q-MCFP-1. Reference instance.
Profit-target gate inside mode_historical. ALREADY ROUNDED at 2 dp. Composite per §0.5 (4); first operator (`outcome == "pass"`) is `==` and excluded. Reference instance.

delta_pp computed at line 98 as abs(pass_rate - 0.9809) * 100 — subtraction at non-equal magnitudes (pass_rate ~0.98 vs 0.9809; both pass-rate aggregations). Drift band gate that halts Notion-post when MC drift exceeds 2pp from anchor. Risk-control-adjacent (operational halt; not live trade gate). LHS is a percentage-point delta (not money, not ratio in the dd-fraction sense) — closest to `other` per §4 ("does not fit cleanly"). RHS is a literal in percentage-point units. Boundary noise unlikely at 2.0 threshold given subtraction inputs are sample means at p~0.98.

findings.expression: both_below = all(r["pass_rate"] < PASS_RATE_FLOOR for r in available)
findings.notes: Regime-check revert-trigger gate per ADR 2026-05-08-dd-trigger-c2-relock. pass_rate is computed at line 142 as passes / total (integer/integer cast to float). RHS is a hard-coded ratio floor. Gates a substantive risk-control action: if both windows fall below, recommends reverting DD_TRIGGER 0.015 -> 0.010 (C2 -> C0). pass_rate division is non-equal magnitudes (e.g. 9500/10000) — ULP-class noise possible but rare at this scale. Per §4 classification: ratio, gates a serious risk-control action. Recommended treatment per ADR ratio rule: round(x,6).

concerns: Precision-rule mismatch (cross-cutting): accounts.py:43/45/162 compare a ratio-typed property (dd_remaining_pct) that is rounded to 2 dp at the property level, not the canonical 6 dp the ADR specifies for ratios. The 2 dp treatment matches the in-file precedent (round(x, 2) inside dd_remaining_pct/target_remaining at lines 32/38) because dd_limit_pct itself lives at 0.01-pp granularity (e.g. 5.0, 1.5) — but the ADR's ratio-rule recommended treatment is 6 dp. Parent (claude.ai) should resolve whether dd_remaining_pct qualifies as a ratio (apply 6 dp) or as a percentage-point quantum where 2 dp is the natural fineness. This is a precision-rule scope question, not a survey rule renegotiation. Flagged here per §5 (6) discipline.

Already-fixed reference instances: 6 of the 17 findings are sites already rounded per the canonical PR #53 / Q-MCFP-1 pattern (dd_protection.py:92; portfolio_mc.py:198,203,205,217,501,510). They are included per §2 ("every site emits a row") as reference baselines for the pattern. If the parent's disposition rule counts only un-fixed sites, the un-fixed count is 4 (dd_protection.py:202, dd_protection.py:205, plus 2 `unclear` sites at dd_protection.py:295 and time_to_pass.py:204). The `dd_remaining_pct` family (accounts.py:43/45/162) is already-rounded at 2 dp; whether it counts as fixed depends on resolution of concern #1. Parent should apply the §9 disposition rule with this distinction explicit.

Out-of-scope import flag: portfolio_mc.py imports DD_TRIGGER and DD_SCALE from dd_protection.py (line 31-34) and shares the same trigger comparison semantics in two sim-class sites (already rounded). No cross-repo imports of firm_rules in scope per §0.5 (2) — confirmed (only same-repo accounts.py imports RISK_TIERS).

analysis/oanda_stage1/ and weekly_review_feeder/ surveyed and produced null findings per §0 conditional. analysis/oanda_stage1/ is research/Stage-1 hypothesis-generation code with statistical/signal-qualification comparisons (p < 0.05, |effect| < cost-floor, match_rate < 0.98), not risk-control gates. weekly_review_feeder/compute.py contains MC-placement bucketing (realized_pnl < p10/p50/p90) which is categorization for Notion reporting — does not gate live trade, position size, or account state.
